description: Official TypeScript/JavaScript client and CLI for the Sonilo API — generate music and sound effects from text or video.
rules: Read the API key from the SONILO_API_KEY environment variable by default; only pass { apiKey } explicitly when the caller has a reason to override it.

For text-to-music and video-to-music: use client.textToMusic.generate() / client.videoToMusic.generate() (streaming) for short synchronous tracks. Use submit() + client.tasks.wait() (async mode) whenever the caller needs outputFormat: "wav", isolateVocals, preserveSpeech, or ducking — the streaming path does not support these.

For text-to-sfx and video-to-sfx, generation is always async: submit() returns a task_id, and generate() (or tasks.wait()) polls it to completion. There is no streaming variant for these two endpoints.

video-to-sound (client.videoToSound) and video-to-video-sound (client.videoToVideoSound) generate a combined music + SFX track and are async only. They share the same params (musicPrompt, sfxPrompt, segments, preserveSpeech, ducking); video-to-sound returns the mixed audio and video-to-video-sound returns a re-hosted video with the track muxed in. The final file is result.output_url (output_type tells you audio vs video).
ducking is default-ON server-side for the video-to-sound endpoints: leave it unset to keep it on and pass ducking: false only to opt out — never send an explicit false just to mean "default".

Result media (audio.url, or output_url for the combined sound endpoints) is a short-lived presigned URL, not the API's own domain — download it directly with the SDK's download() helper or fetch(); do not send the Authorization header to it.

Wrap calls in try/catch for AuthenticationError (401), PaymentRequiredError (402), RateLimitError (429, has retryAfter), and TaskFailedError (task reached status: "failed") rather than a single generic error branch — callers usually want to handle these differently.
video/videoUrl parameters accept exactly one of the two, never both and never neither — check for that before constructing a request.
